title: daucus gingidium subsp. fontanesii
intro: Daucus gingidium subsp. fontanesii, also known as Fontanes' wild carrot or wild carrot, is a subspecies of plant belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae) and is native to the Mediterranean region. Its medicinal properties are attributed to its high content of essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Daucus gingidium subsp. fontanesii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support immune function, as well as its traditional use in treating various conditions such as fever and respiratory issues. Daucus gingidium subsp. fontanesii is traditionally prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is also used in the production of essential oils and herbal supplements.
